classObjective: Solve systems containing decimals, fractions, and special angle or digit relationships and analyze and compare solution methods for systems of equations.
workedExamples: 7-2, Example 2 — Applications of Systems of Equations (continued)
warmUp: Solve: 1/2 x + 1/3 y = 4 and x - y = 3
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: fractional and decimal coefficients; complementary angle problems; number theory applications; method comparison
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice solving systems with fractions and special relationships and comparing methods
independentPractice: Practice problems solving systems with special coefficients and analyzing solution methods
assessment: Exit ticket: solve a system involving complementary angles using substitution
capReflection: How does dealing with fractions or decimals in systems change your approach to substitution?
